name: Marinated Teriyaki Aubergine
description: Sweet, sticky and salty teriyaki-marinated aubergine, griddled until charred and served over rice.

ingredients.name: sushi rice
ingredients.notes: or short grain rice; quantity to taste

steps.title: Make the marinade
steps.content: Whisk {0002}, {0003}, {0004}, {0005}, {0006} and {0007} together in a bowl until the sugar is dissolved.

steps.title: Coat the aubergine
steps.content: Cut {0001} into small chunks and place in a large bowl. Pour the marinade over the top, stir well to coat, and set aside.

steps.title: Marinate
steps.content: Leave to marinate for at least 20 minutes, stirring a few times.

steps.title: Griddle the aubergine
steps.content: Place a griddle pan on the hob over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the marinated {0001}. Pour over any excess marinade while cooking. You may need to cook in two batches, keeping the first batch warm on a low heat in the oven. Cook for a few minutes on each side until brown and starting to char from the griddle lines.

steps.title: Serve
steps.content: Serve immediately with {0008} or as a side. Top with {0009} and {0010}.

notes:
Mix it up by adding different vegetables or tofu for extra protein.

Source nutrition figures (per serving, yield 2): 342 kcal, 10g protein, 56g carbohydrates, 7g fat. These likely include the rice accompaniment and are not carried into the macro fields.

_fixture_notes.external_ref: Supplied slug 'marinated-teriyaki-eggplant' used verbatim; not re-derived. Note: slug uses American English 'eggplant' rather than British English 'aubergine' — preserved verbatim as instructed.
_fixture_notes.name: Source title is 'Marinated Teriyaki Eggplant'. Display name preserved as written; 'eggplant' not converted to 'aubergine' in the name field, as the spec's British English rule applies to slug generation, not the display name. Flagged for operator to amend if preferred.
_fixture_notes.base_servings: Source header states YIELD: 2-3; nutrition block states YIELD: 2. Header is the canonical yield statement. Range convention applied: base_servings set to 2, range noted. Nutrition block yield (2) happens to match lower bound.
_fixture_notes.prep_time_minutes: Labelled PREP TIME field: 20 minutes. Used at face value. The 20-minute marinade rest (step 3) accounts for the majority of this; the spec's in-session passive waits rule is superseded by the labelled field, which is used as stated.
_fixture_notes.cook_time_minutes: Labelled COOK TIME field: 10 minutes. Used at face value. Stated step duration in the griddle step is 'a few minutes on each side' — non-standard, not a numeric duration. Labelled field governs.
_fixture_notes.macros: Source provides per-serving nutrition figures. Carbohydrates (56g per serving) strongly suggests rice is included in the calculation — rice is a to-serve accompaniment. Per spec, figures that include accompaniments are excluded from macro fields and placed in notes.
_fixture_notes.cup_units: Source uses cup measurements (1/2 cup soy sauce, 1/4 cup mirin). Cups are a valid unit per the spec template. No conversion applied; cup units preserved as stated.
_fixture_notes.ginger_length: Source specifies '1 inch ginger'. Length descriptor treated as non-standard quantity: amount null, unit null, notes '1-inch piece'. Leading-prep form in source ('grated' follows the ingredient) is already trailing-clause form; convention satisfied.
_fixture_notes.brown_sugar_range: Source specifies '1-2 tbsp brown sugar'. Range convention: lower bound 1 tbsp, range noted in ingredient notes.
_fixture_notes.sushi_rice_alternative: Source lists 'Sushi/short grained rice'. Inline-alternatives convention: sushi rice taken as canonical (first-named), short grain rice noted. No quantity stated — functional to-serve amount.
_fixture_notes.dietary_restrictions: Soy: soy sauce. Sesame: sesame seeds. No gluten-containing ingredients identified in the main recipe (soy sauce could contain gluten depending on brand — e.g. standard soy sauce vs tamari). Conservative default: gluten not flagged as the spec says restrictions describe what the dish contains, and no wheat-based ingredient is explicitly listed. Flagged for operator review — if standard soy sauce is assumed, gluten should be added.
_fixture_notes.meal_types: Source categorised as 'Mains' and notes it can be served as a side. Assigned dinner and side.
_fixture_notes.no_derived_components: Batch mode — no derived components proposed. The teriyaki marinade is a plausible derivation candidate as a reusable sauce. Flagged for operator review.
